NXT-64497.ac:
* Default Academic Year set to current year on page load.
* Editing of Federal rates locked to the Customer Support AND Tech Support admin roles.
* User can set reimbursement rates per:
** State Category (Contiguous, Alaska, Hawaii)
** per academic year
** for both Federal and State types
* User can set rates for Centers
** by Meal Type (Breakfast, Lunch/Supper, Supplement)
** by Eligibility (Free, Reduced, Paid)
* User can set rates for At-Risk After School Meals
** by Meal Types: Breakfast, Lunch/Supper, Snacks
** by Eligibility (Free)
* User can set rates for Day Care Homes
** by Tiers: Tier I, Tier II
** by Meal Types: Breakfast, Lunch/Supper, Supplement
* User can set Administrative Rates for Sponsors of Day Care Homes
** by number of homes: Initial 50 (1 through 50), Next 150 (51 through 200), Next 800 (201 through 1000), Additional (1001 and over)
** just 1 reimbursement rate for each range of homes
NXT-64497.desc:
As Dana the Director I want to see an estimate on my reimbursement claim - and I need to have proper reimbursement rates to do that. The current entries do not match with actual CACFP programs. 

h3. *Reimbursement Rates*

!image-20250718-181558.png|width=50%,alt="image-20250718-181558.png"!

Current screenshot above. Lot of meal types in there that shouldn’t be.

*Requirements*

* Default the Academic Year selector to the current year.
* Lock the editing of Federal rates to the Customer Support and Tech Support admin roles.
* User can set reimbursement rates for the following for the typical State Category (Contiguous, Alaska, Hawaii):
** Cash-in-Lieu of Commodities
*** Flat amount, money, 2 decimals. Example: $0.30
** Centers (Adult Day Care Centers and Child Care Centers)
*** Meal Types: Breakfast, ‘Lunch and Supper’, Supplement
**** Individual rates for Free, Reduced, Paid
** At Risk After School Meals
*** Meal Types: Breakfast, Lunch/Supper, Snacks
**** 1 rate only, no sub-eligibility.
** Day Care Homes
*** Tiers: Tier I, Tier II
**** Meal Types: Breakfast, Lunch/Supper, Supplement
** Administrative Rates for Sponsors of Day Care Homes
*** Initial 50 (1 through 50), Next 150 (51 through 200), Next 800 (201 through 1000), Additional (1001 and over)
**** 1 rate only for each

NXT-64498.ac:
* User can run the Activity Report for CACFP.
* User is not shown every possible CACFP Meal Type in the report.
** Nice to have: use Program Configuration.
** Alternative: provide a Meal Type dropdown just for CACFP reports.
* User can see the PowerBI report with meals, sales, and a reimbursement estimate.
NXT-64498.rn:
The Activity report has been updated as part of our continued rollout of support for CACFP. Reimbursement calculations are also possible for certain facility types.

You will need to check and set the following configuration screens to see data in the report:

* Program Configuration (System > Management)
* Site Configuration (System > Sites & Users)
* Reimbursement Rates (System > Management)

Meal and money amounts for all facility types will be shown, but the report will calculate reimbursement amounts for only the following facility types: Adult Day Care Center, At-Risk After School Care Center, and Child Care Center.
NXT-64498.desc:
As Dana the Director I want to run the Activity report for my CACFP program so I can get an overview on meal counts, sales, and an estimate on reimbursement.

Currently the Activity report does not provide support for CACFP.

*Requirements*

* User can select CACFP from the Programs dropdown.
* User can run the Activity report for CACFP and see the typical information, but focused on the CACFP meal types.
** Nice to have: Only show the configured meals (from Program Configuration) for the selected site(s) + date(s).
*** Reason: There are 6 different CACFP Meals, and most districts only run ~2 meals on average. Lot of blank space if we show all 6.
*** Alternative: Add a Meal Type dropdown to the report page when CACFP is selected, and let the user select which meal types to show/not show.
** Reimbursement column will need to check the Site Config to determine what rate(s) to pull for calculations. See the table below.
*** If not set, default to using the Centers reim. rates.
*** If more than one selected, use the first that applies.
*** Do not run reimbursement calculation if set to a Facility Type that we’re ignoring.

!image-20250721-204127.png|width=730,height=490,alt="image-20250721-204127.png"!



||*Facility Type*||*Options*||*Rates to use*||
|Adult Day Care Center|Yes|Centers > Free/Reduced/Paid|
|At-Risk After School Care Center|N/A|At-Risk > Free|
|Child Care Center|Yes|Centers > Free/Reduced/Paid|
|Day Care Home|Ignore| |
|Head Start|Ignore| |
|Homeless/Emergency Shelter|Ignore| |
|Outside School Hours Care Center|Ignore| |

We have also made some changes to how the Edit Check report interacts with CEP/Provision 2 sites. Only the meal Total columns will be flagged for potential edit checks, not the individual free/reduced/paid columns. NXT-65294.summary: ACC - Labor Hours Feature - New Page
NXT-65294.desc:
A key metric for districts like any business operation is the ability to track labor costs and efficiency.  In order for SchoolCafe to offer this feature, we will need to implement a way to store, update, and manage labor hour entry.

Ideally there are 2 way we can support entry: One for the standard(quick) method and another for precision.

This boils down to 2 entirely different storage structures which user need to be able to seamlessly choose between based on whether they can find the time/effort worth revealing this key metric through later support for data visualizations.

This story contains the need to create the 2 storage structures(determined via in-page side bar configuration) and how they are represented on the page. 
Standard Entry Grid mockup


!image-20250812-175348.png|width=839,height=652,alt="image-20250812-175348.png"!

Columns:
Role(pre configured), Number of Staff(entry), Hours Worked Daily(pre configured), Days in the Period(based on existence of served meal or operating days, TEXT BOX ENTERABLE), Total Staff Hours (sum)

Precise Entry Grid mockup

!image-20250812-174615.png|width=843,height=639,alt="image-20250812-174615.png"!

Columns:
Site(based on filter), Days(based on filter), Categories (pre configured, order by same order defined as well as user orderable within the grid), Total (sum)

The grid support pagination as it can quickly grow based on site and date range selection.

Page (Labor Hours) will go above “Reports” in the accountability module

Standard page permission for this feature.



Use Date control from Accountability Transactions page. (uses period)


SIDE BAR options:

To handle the need for configuring the labor hours feature to the 2 main ways of use, we will be implementing an on page side bar settings.
We will need to include a new permission to be able to access this.

Add new user permission: Labor Hours - Settings. (View, Edit, Add, Delete)
When view - user can view side bar

When edit - user can select the entry method and Meal Equivalent configurations.
When add - user can add new roles/categories
When delete - user can “deactivate” roles/categories

Add drop down for Academic year in the settings bar to view each year’s configuration (default to current Academic year) at the top of the slide out.

Two entry methods configuration:
{color:#ff5630}*_Standard_*{color}
(when clicking “add”) Allows configuration of either Full or Part Time Staff Flags “Role”
Allow configuration of either Full or Part Time Staff Flags “FT/PT” (Check box with column header “Full Time?”, Checked = FT, Unchecked = PT)
Number of Hours
Active or Inactive Status
Action to Deactivate/Reactivate.
Ordered by creation date.

Button to calculate number of days in period.
Provide a button near the top of the grid to generate number of days in the period columns by summing all of the days a meal (breakfast or lunch) was served.



Stored by Academic Year where previous configuration is carried over.  (year begin script or on creation of new academic years)

Splash note:
Use these settings to control how Labor Hour Entry and Meal Equivalents Ratios are utilized.
Standard Entry - Simple table layout to record number of hours worked by defining and then entering number of shifts worked.
Precise Entry - In depth entry for configurable categories recorded by hours worked.
Meal Equivalents - Configure by modifying how many units required (U) to make how many equivalents of a meal (M). Used in meal per labor hour reporting.

Default Roles
Full Time Staff 8 hours
Part Time Staff 6 hours
Part Time Staff 4 hours

!image-20250812-201624.png|width=416,height=629,alt="image-20250812-201624.png"!



{color:#ff5630}*_Precise_*{color}

(when clicking “add”) Allows configuration of either Full or Part Time Staff Flags “FT/PT”  (Check box with column header “Full Time?”, Checked = FT, Unchecked = PT)
Labor Category
Active or Inactive Status
Action to Deactivate/Reactivate.
Ordered by creation date.



Stored by Academic Year where previous configuration is carried over. (year begin script or on creation of new academic years)

Default Roles
Manager
Kitchen Staff
Cashier

Users can modify default roles text

!image-20250812-201557.png|width=406,height=631,alt="image-20250812-201557.png"!

*_Tooltips for Grid Headers_*
*_Labor Categories_*
{color:#ff5630}*Standard*{color}
Role “{color:#ff991f}Added Role for hours tracking{color}”
Role Type “{color:#ff991f}Used in report grouping{color}”
Hours “{color:#ff991f}Use for tracking sum of work hour shifts{color}”
Active/Inactive Column “{color:#ff991f}Use to show in Main Grid{color}”
{color:#ff5630}*Precise*{color}
Category “{color:#ff991f}Added Labor Category for Hours tracking{color}”
Category Type “{color:#ff991f}Used in report grouping{color}”
Active/Inactive Column “{color:#ff991f}Use to show in Main Grid{color}”
{color:#36b37e}*Meal Equivalents*{color}
Ratio 1 “{color:#ff991f}Number of Meals from Meal Type to Equal Set Meal Equivalents{color}”
Ratio 2 “{color:#ff991f}Number of Meal Equivalents{color}”
Conversion Factor “{color:#ff991f}Equals Ratio 2 divided by Ratio 1, Used for reporting equations{color}”



A user can switch Entry method at any time without losing data entered.

For both entry methods:
If no Role/Category is configured for the selected academic year.
Display no grid and a message as a place holder:
”There are no Roles/Categories configured for this year. Please configure at least 1 within the settings in order to display the grid”

{color:#36b37e}*_Meal Equivalents configuration_*{color}
Remove Meal Equivalents widget from the dashboard
Add an include flag for all meal types (order by program (SNP, SFSP, CACFP), Breakfast/Lunch/Snack/Supper)
Number Produced to Equal
Number of Meal Equivalents
Conversion Factor displays ratio in decimal form (calculated)
Default all to 1:1
Display Edit Button to modify ratios

*For Non-Program food sales
Use 2 entry fields to store float values up to 4 decimal points.
(this will later be used in a report equations as $ Total / (value 1 + value 2)
-Add Tool Tip: Used to convert all non-program sales into MEQs. Calculated by dividing $Sales total by (the annual federal reimbursement rate for a free lunch + USDA Foods value).

*Note:*

Insights Workspace is using this data - we need to be sure the location remains the same and the API remains intact.

*Mini-Demo Discussion (9/3/25)*

* Labor Hours
** Delete will not be available.
** Include column to be renamed to Status, Yes/No to Active/Inactive respectively.
** Adding a Minutes option for Standard entry.
* Meal Equivalents:
** Include option can be removed.
** Categories should be the same as the current Meal Equivalents on the Workspace dashboard.
** Decimals need to go out to 2 for the Ratios and the Conversion Factor, round to nearest.
** Non-Program Food Sales → Show the NSLP Lunch reimbursement rate for the first $ amount, user can edit the additional amount. Go to 4 decimals for this one.
* Role Permissions still needing to be done.

NXT-65296.desc:
Story to contain data entry behavior of main grid for both entry methods.
Standard Method
User can immediately edit values but must click save to commit changes.
User is presented a +- text field that only accepts numbers and can be stepped + or - by 1.
User can click Cancel to discard changes.
Grid updates Displayed month based on selected date.
Data is only for a month at a time.

User is presented text field that only accepts numbers up to a tenth “00.0”.
User can click Cancel to discard changes recalls last saved data.
Grid updates available text boxes based on selected date and site.
Grid can remember change after pagination.
Grid supports ADA compliant field tabbing.
Provide a checkbox to highlight the background of Cells that have no data (“Highlight Missing Counts”) Remembers even after filter change.
Totals update after saving.

Highlight any text field where a change is detected.

NXT-65332.summary: ACC - Attendance Feature - ADA - ADP Report
NXT-65332.desc:
Another common metric that districts find useful is the Average Daily Participation (ADP). With the implementation of attendance tracking, we can utilize the entered data in this stand alone report.

Add New page report in Accountability > Reports

Columns
Site Name
Meal Type - shows only configured meal types (SNP only)
Total Attendance/ OR / Total Approved Counts - (when use attendance counts is selected on the report option shows the sum of entered counts from attendance ELSE shows total Enrollment sum per site selected in the report for the selected date range. STUDENTS ONLY)
”# of Serving Days [DATE RANGE 00/00/00 - 00/00/00]” - Selected Date range in the report where at least 1 meal was served
Average Daily Attendance - [Total Attendance / # of Serving Days where a meal was served]
Total Meals Served [Total meals served for selected date range]
Average Meals Served Per Day - [Total meals served for selected date range / # of Serving Days where a meal was served]
Average Daily Participation - [Average Daily Meals / Average Daily Attendance]

Report Filters
Site (Multi Select)
Date Range (standard control)
Meal Type (Multi Select)
Use Attendance Counts for ADP calculations (check box Default unchecked)
When check = Use entered attendance Count
When uncheck = Use Approved Count

JOSH - {color:#4c9aff}Add Friendly Paragraph explaining what it is and how it is calculated{color}

* {color:#4c9aff}Friendly messages for when report can yield blank (no attendance counts, NO SNP config, etc){color}
- Looks like there is no attendance data for the date(s) selected. Please enter Attendance counts for the date(s) or run without using attendance counts.
- No Program configuration exists for this year.
* {color:#4c9aff}Report Name and short description{color}
”ADA/ADP report.”
”Average Daily Attendance and Average Daily Participation report used for gathering insight on student participation by day and meal type.”
* {color:#4c9aff}formula report footnotes{color}

Legend:
{color:#ff991f}Changes based on checkbox{color}
{color:#4c9aff}When{color} “Use Attendance Counts for ADP calculations” is checked
{color:#4c9aff}Display{color}
Total Attendance = Sum of Attendance counts entered for selected date range.
{color:#4c9aff}Else{color}
Total Approved Counts = Sum of Enrolled by day for selected date range. 

{color:#ff991f}Static{color}
Average Daily Attendance = Total Attendance/Approved divided by # of Serving Days
Average Meals Served Per Day = Total Meals Served divided by # of Serving Days
Average Daily Participation = Average Daily Meals / Average Daily Attendance

Show report selection on the report.

{color:#97a0af}*Other report logic:*{color}

NXT-66332.summary: ACC - Attendance Feature - Monthly Entry - Main Grid and Filters
NXT-66332.desc:
In order to support the ability to utilize accurate attendance counts within reporting as well as meet NC requirements the attendance page will need several changes.

To accommodate a relaxed entry method of tracking ADA and Attendance Monthly we will be taking the existing monthly entry method on the attendance page and introducing a few quality of life improvements.

When Monthly Entry Method is selected

First we will be implementing separate tracking for SNP, CACFP, and SFSP counts.

Implement individual grid storage tabs for SNP, CACFP, and SFSP
*Top Filters*

Site filter (Allow multi - site select for site control)
Academic Year filter
Months” Filter ( July - December, January - June)

*For the monthly grid view*
- Display 6 months at a time starting from July
- Shrink the size of the individual column and text box sizes to comfortably fit 6 months.(keep existing text box control behavior)
- On Page load Text boxes are immediately editable

*Buttons*
- Provide a simple back and forward arrow buttons to switch between the 6 month increments. (within the same academic year only)
- Provide pagination for sites (default 10)
- Provide a “Highlight Missing Counts” when click stylizes cells with null values (bold colored border and bold highlighted background)
- Provide a grid export button that simply exports current grid view to excel
-Stylize current month to be highlighted with Bright Border around the Major column Cell

!image-20250908-041657.png|width=849,height=510,alt="image-20250908-041657.png"!

*Role Permissions*

* Standard directory permissions for accessing the page, viewing data, updating, etc.
* Special role permission for adjusting the configuration:
** Name: Attendance - Configuration Options
** Toggle on for the following roles: Director, Central Office, SchoolCafe Administrator, Customer Support + Tech Support

NXT-66333.summary: ACC - Attendance Feature - Monthly Entry Updates and Saving
NXT-66333.desc:
For updating and saving the Monthly Entry method for attendance we will need to do the following.

-Update data only after user clicks save.
-Provide a cancel button that simply clears inputs (shows if the page detects a change in any single textbox)
-Temporarily retain data if user pages sites or months. (only after changing months or pagination for sites, not expected if a new site filter is applied, or entry method change)

*Bulk Entry Tool.*

-Provide a bulk entry tool that takes 2 values and applies those to ALL respective columns for every month after clicking an “Apply”. 

* Provide a warning message before applying) - “Are you sure you wish to set the Average Daily Attendance/Attendance Factor for ALL months with the entered values?” (Yes/No)

NXT-66334.summary: ACC - Attendance Feature - Daily Entry - Main Grids and Filters
NXT-66334.desc:
To accommodate a more detailed tracking Attendance Daily we will be providing a daily entry method.

* Implement separate tracking tabs for SNP, CACFP, and SFSP counts.

Main Page for daily entry
- Implement individual grid storage tabs for SNP, CACFP, and SFSP
- Provide a Site filter (Allow multi-site select for site control)
- Academic Year filter.
- Date Filter (Standard Date filter) (date range max = 31)
- Provide an apply button to update grid

Shows when entry method “Daily” is selected

For the daily grid view

* Display Main Grid Header that show “Daily [Program] Attendance Entry For [00/00 - 00/00]

* Display columns for Site(combined with site code) and Date for selected range (displaying abbreviated day name)
* Display the current week Starting with Sunday by default.
* Grid “day” column area expands or shrinks depending on date selection (up to 31 days)
* Grid has horizontal scrolling for days if grid gets too large
* Grid supports pagination for sites (default 10)
* Provide a “Highlight missing counts” check box where when checks stylizes the borders of check boxes with null values in a bright red. (default unchecked)
* Provide a “Include weekends” which when checked displays all Saturdays and Sundays contained within selected date range (default unchecked)
* Provide a grid export button that simply exports current grid view to excel
* On Page load Text boxes are immediately editable
* Stylize current day to be highlighted with Bright Border around the Major column Cell(shown in mockup)
* acceptable input (whole numbers only, zero is acceptable, limit to 6 digits)
* {color:#ff991f}New after PO review* Add the visual change indicator same as monthly view (shows red dot when change is detected){color}

Terminal lists now default to no selection, treat “none” as “all,” and display active terminals first with clear active/inactive indicators. NXT-67218.summary: ACC - Labor Hours Feature - Release Merge / Release Testing
NXT-67218.rn:
The new Labor hours feature provides a space to track Labor for accurate Meals Per Labor Hour tracking as well as within other reporting's.

On first time set up, or at anytime you would like, users can select between two modes of entry; Standard and Precise. In order to select mode, simply click on the text/tab within the settings slide out and click save.


!image-20251124-184525.png|width=537,alt="image-20251124-184525.png"!


Standard
Standard method allows the configuration of generic roles and predefined shift hours. Once set, users can enter the number of staff who worked the shift and page will multiply by the number of days in the period to give Total Staff hours.
This method works best for operations where hours are consistent or users would like a quick way to enter hours for an entire month period.


!image-20251124-184511.png|width=537,alt="image-20251124-184511.png"!


Precise
The Precise method allows user to define specific roles to track hours per day in a period. Users can enter down to the minute or stick to hours only by choosing so with grid filters.


!image-20251124-184757.png|width=537,alt="image-20251124-184757.png"!


Meal Equivalents
Also within the settings menu is the ability to set Meal Equivalents. This configuration will be used to calculate Meals per labor hour by multiplying the set conversion factor against the specific meal type meal counts. So for example if I have a ratio of 3 Student breakfast meals to equal 2 meals created (a conversion factor of 0.67) when calculating meals per labor hour for Student breakfasts, the sum of student breakfasts will be multiplied by 0.67. For Non-Program Food Sales, Meal per labor hour will be calculated by dividing the total revenue collected from Non-Program Food sales by the conversion factor.


!image-20251124-185125.png|width=537,alt="image-20251124-185125.png"!

KPI Benchmarks.
This space will be used to input a goal to compare real values to per month per site for Meals Per Labor hour values. This input is not used in any calculation but simply displayed on the MPLH report as well as made available for Workspace.

MPLH report.


!image-20251124-190121.png|width=537,alt="image-20251124-190121.png"!

The Meals Per Labor Hour Report will take the meal counts across several meal types as well as A La carte sales and convert it into Meal Equivalents using the configured conversions factors. The report will then be able to display a sites total Meals per labor hour value per site per date that the report is generated.
Note that this report will share the same filters as the page.


----


Special permissions required for KPI Benchmark access
General permissions for Page access and Setting slide out access.

NXT-67408.desc:
As Dana the Director I want better support for Reimbursement Claims - a way to attach documents, reports, and a rollback feature.

*requirements*

* User has a guided workflow process for creating claims.
** Step 1: User can select programs.
** Step 2: User can trigger rollups.
** Step 3: User can generate reports and have them attached to the claim.
** Step 4: User sees a summary.
** More info on steps below.
* Requested actions are taken care of, and then the User is informed when the claim(s) are ready.

!image-20251013-210242.png|width=442,alt="image-20251013-210242.png"!



----

Step 1 - Programs

* User can see the programs they are actively using for the selected period. (Check Program Configuration)
* User can select whichever programs they want out of the available list. If only 1 is available, we should select it for them.

Step 2 - Rollups

* User can see if rollups are needed / suggested.
** We flag this on the Report Rollups page if any data > last rollup date exists.
* User can ask for reports to be triggered for the period.

Step 3 - Supporting Documentation

* User can select reports to be run for the period.
* Always run Detailed reports for the selected programs.
* They should be attached to the claim afterwards.

Step 4 - Summary

* User can review their selections and start claim creation.

NXT-68314.summary: Add Native System Info Module to Provide CPU, Memory, Storage & Temperature Metrics
NXT-68314.desc:
h2. User Story

*As a* system administrator managing School Cafe TV devices  
*I want* to monitor device performance metrics in real-time from the dashboard  
*So that* I can proactively identify and resolve performance issues before they impact menu display

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] Dashboard can request and receive CPU information including processor model, cores, and usage percentage
* [ ] Dashboard can request and receive memory statistics including total, available, used, and usage percentage
* [ ] Dashboard can view CPU temperature when available on the device
* [ ] Dashboard can monitor storage capacity and usage
* [ ] Device responds to socket events REQ_MEM_INFO and REQ_CPU_INFO with accurate data
* [ ] System information is read directly from Android native APIs (/proc, thermal zones, ActivityManager)
* [ ] Temperature readings are displayed in Celsius with availability status
* [ ] All metrics are accurate and match Android system settings

h2. Technical Notes

* Native module implemented in Java using /proc filesystem and Android APIs
* No special Android permissions required
* Socket integration uses existing UNICAST_MESSAGE pattern
* TypeScript interfaces provide type safety for all system info data
* Error handling for devices without temperature sensors

h2. Definition of Done

* Code reviewed and approved
* Native module tested on multiple Android versions (API 21-35)
* Socket events tested with dashboard integration
* Documentation added for module usage
* No performance degradation in menu display

NXT-69980.summary: POS - Credit Cards > Square Integration > Square Transaction Feature
NXT-69980.ac:
When a Square terminal is configured the credit card tab on the “PAY” pop up is replaced with Square functionality.
POS terminals that are not configured for Square will see the existing credit card tab + workflow.
When clicked >ExpressPoint will initialize a Square transaction that can then be completed on the Square Device.
When a credit card transaction is completed a user is presented with a brief green check on the pop up which will then auto close the pop up and display success message toast.
Users are presented with Helper text on each step of the Square Transaction.
Users can cancel at any time even if it means we handle delays on the backend.
A timer is presented which will automatically timeout/cancel the transaction if nothing has been returned by the square terminal. (Default 3 Minutes)
Users can initialize another Square transaction after completion or even after one has been canceled.
Users are alerted with a toast message of the status of a Square transaction after completion, canceled, or timeout.
Complete Transaction Button is disabled during a credit card transaction for Square(auto closes with success message when finished)
UI Dollar and Number buttons are disabled during a credit card transaction for Square (reenables if user cancels)
If a user clicks Cash or Check tab, Cancel the Credit Card Transactions.
Log all Cancel interactions for future reporting.
NXT-69980.rn: When in a session to initiate a square transaction simply click the Pay button and navigate to the Card tab. Once on the card tab you can initiate a transaction on the Square device by clicking the complete transaction button. This will initiate the transaction on the square device and once completed the transaction will automatically finish on the POS and move on to the next in queue. If needed you can cancel a transaction at any time, however, users must be aware of any discrepancies that could happen as a result of clicking cancel within a few seconds of a patron going through with a credit card payment on the physical square device. Cancelling a transaction also takes additional time to resolve before allowing a user to initiate another. A timeout timer will also be visible to cancel an initialized square transaction for security purposes. Payments to add additional funds to a patrons account is not supported by this feature and only payment of the exact amount are possible.

NXT-70454.summary: SCTV+ - [Big Card Menu Planning Widget ] Add 2×3 Column Support and Dynamic Scaling to Big Card Type (Max 5 Items per Widget)
NXT-70454.ac:
h2.  Acceptance Criteria

h3. Layout & Configuration

# The Big Card Menu Planning widget supports a *2×3 column layout* per column.
# The system allows *vertical and horizontal stacking* without layout breaking.
# Each widget supports *up to 5 menu items*.
# The layout renders correctly when:
#* Only 1 widget is present
#* Multiple widgets are present
#* The maximum supported configuration is used

----

h3. Dynamic Scaling

# All Menu Item View components dynamically resize based on:
#* Number of widgets displayed
#* Number of items inside the widget
#* Stacking direction
# The following components scale proportionally and remain readable:
#* Image frame
#* Food components
#* Calories
#* Title
#* Description
# No content overlaps, truncation issues, or UI breaking occurs in any supported configuration.

----

h3. Validation & Stability

# The system prevents adding widget configurations that would cause rendering or layout instability.
# Adding or removing widgets dynamically updates the layout without requiring a page refresh.
# No console errors or runtime rendering errors occur when using supported configurations.

----

h3. Performance

# Rendering performance remains stable when using the maximum supported configuration (2×3 layout with 5 items per widget).
NXT-70454.desc:
h3. Story Description

This PR enhances the *Big Card Menu Planning widget* by introducing support for a *2×3 column layout* along with full dynamic scaling capabilities.

The goal is to improve layout flexibility, increase scalability, and ensure rendering stability across different widget and item configurations.

----

h3.  Key Enhancements

* Added support for *2×3 layout per column* (in addition to the previously supported 2×2).
* Enabled *vertical and horizontal widget stacking* with dynamic layout behavior.
* Each widget supports *up to 5 items per widget* in the standard configuration.
* Implemented validation rules to prevent invalid widget combinations that could cause rendering or UI layout issues.
* Improved overall scalability of the Big Card layout to better handle larger content sets.

----

h3.  Component-Level Improvements

All components inside the *Menu Item View* were updated to be fully flexible and scalable, including:

* Image frame
* Food components
* Calories
* Title
* Description

Each component dynamically adjusts based on:

* Number of widgets displayed
* Number of items inside the widget
* Stacking direction (vertical or horizontal)

This ensures proper spacing, alignment, readability, and visual consistency whether the layout contains fewer widgets or higher-density configurations.

----

h3.  Scalability Improvements

* Menu items are now fully scalable, allowing users to add the maximum supported number of items per widget.
* Layout behavior adapts automatically to maintain UI stability and prevent overflow or rendering issues.
* The system now better supports larger and more complex widget configurations without breaking the UI.

----

h3.  Outcome

Deliver a more flexible, scalable, and stable Big Card Menu Planning widget that supports expanded configurations while maintaining clean and consistent rendering across all supported layouts.

NXT-70455.summary: SCTV+ - Enforce Strict Column Placement Rules for Menu Planning and Menu List Widgets-Web Dashboard
NXT-70455.ac:
h1. Acceptance Criteria

h2. Menu Planning Widget Rules

# A user can add a *first Menu Planning widget* to a column successfully.
# A user can add a *second Menu Planning widget* to the same column successfully.
# Attempting to add a *third Menu Planning widget* to the same column is blocked and shows the orange warning modal.
# Attempting to add Menu Planning widgets to a *third column* on the same slide is blocked and shows the orange warning modal.
# A column containing a Menu Planning widget cannot exceed *2 total rows*.

----

h2. Menu List Widget Rules

# A Menu List widget can be added only to a *completely empty column*.
# Attempting to add a Menu List widget to a column that contains any other widget is blocked and shows the blue warning modal.
# Attempting to add an empty slot (Top/Bottom) to a column that already contains a Menu List widget is blocked and shows the blue warning modal.
# The system does not allow bypassing the rule under any condition (no “warn but allow” behavior).

----

h2. Items Limit Enforcement (Menu Planning)

# When a slide contains more than one Menu Planning widget, the item count is capped at *5*.
# When a Menu Planning widget shares its column with another widget, the item count is capped at *5*.
# If a user attempts to increase items beyond 5:

* The value is automatically clamped to 5.
* The appropriate warning modal is displayed.

# The cap is enforced consistently via:

* Slider interaction
* Manual numeric input
* On blur validation

----

h2. Stability & Validation

# No invalid layout configuration can be saved.
# No rendering errors occur on device slides when using supported configurations.
# No console errors occur during widget addition, removal, or item updates.
NXT-70455.desc:
This update enforces strict per-column placement rules for *Menu Planning* and *Menu List* widgets to prevent layout conflicts and rendering issues on device slides.

The goal is to improve slide stability, eliminate invalid configurations, and ensure consistent rendering behavior across all supported layouts.

----

h2. Summary of Changes

h3.  Menu Planning Widget (MP)

* Fixed the column row cap logic to count *actual Menu Planning widgets per column*, not total rows.
* Users can add *up to 2 Menu Planning widgets per column*.
* Columns containing a Menu Planning widget are capped at *2 total rows*.
* A maximum of *2 columns per slide* may contain Menu Planning widgets.
* Any violation triggers the existing *orange warning modal*.

----

h3.  Menu List Widget (ML)

* Strengthened the column guard:
** A Menu List widget can only be added to a *completely empty column*.
** No other widgets of any type may exist in that column.
* Removed the previous “warn but allow” behavior — the block is now *absolute*.
* Adding an empty slot (Top/Bottom) to a column that already contains a Menu List widget is *blocked*.
* Violations trigger the existing *blue warning modal* with updated messaging.

----

h3.  Items Limit (Menu Planning)

The number of items in a Menu Planning widget is capped at *5 items* when:

* The slide contains more than one Menu Planning widget, *OR*
* The focused Menu Planning widget shares its column with another widget.

The cap is enforced consistently across:

* Slider changes
* Manual text input
* On blur validation

If a user attempts to exceed the limit, the value is clamped to 5 and the warning modal is shown.

NXT-71603.summary: Square Integrations > Backend change for Credit Cards to separate from cash.
NXT-71603.desc:
Currently in our epic to integrate square to the product we have run into the same problem that PrimeroEdge suffers: Credit cards are fused with Cash sum. To allow for proper accounting Cash must be separate from Card so that card transactions do not create variances in reporting.

It will be fine if credit card amounts create deposit variances but they should not create initial calculated cash variance as credit cards do not count as cash.

Thus,

Credit card sums should not affect the variance or calculated cash and must be completely absent from the backend calculations to avoid additional edge cases down the line.

-Separate credit card sums (including Square and normal credit cards) from cash sum in the rollups

-check to see that credit card sums are not affecting calculated cash or variance.
-Credit card sums are still expected on the deposit and can reflect with a deposit variance.

NXT-71622.summary: Cash Collection Report - Remove Cards from Report
NXT-71622.rn_title: Cash Collection Report - Include Credit Cards
NXT-71622.ac:
All Credit Card sums are removed from all calculations used in this report.
{color:#006644}Update after call for clarity on concerns for functionality:{color}
{color:#ff991f}New Checkbox{color}
Add new report option “Include Credit Cards”
When checked display new column “Total Credit Card Sales” that displays Total Card Sales per day with final Total sum for the site total.

{color:#ff991f}Existing columns{color}
Variance: Remove Credit Cards and rename to “Variance*”

Amount Deposited: Remove Credit Cards, Rename to “Amount Deposited*”

Deposit Variance: Remove Credit Cards, Rename to “Deposit Variance*”

{color:#ff991f}Report Legend{color}

* +*Report Legend*+
* *Variance =* Actual Cash – Calculated Cash
* *Cash Deposit Variance = Amount Deposited - Calculated Cash*
* *Add’l Income -> Additional Income*
* *Cal. Cash -> Calculated Cash (includes checks)*
* * These amount do not contain credit card sales and may differ from other reports that account for credit cards.
* Note: Checks are included in Cash amounts, Credit Cards are not included in Variance or Deposit Variance on this report.
NXT-71622.rn:
To make way for easier use of this report and Square. We have separated Credit Cards from cash entirely. Previously Credit card amounts would mix with the cash columns. We have added a report option “Include Credit Cards”.
When the new report option is checked: a new column “Total Credit Card Sales” will show that displays the Total Card Sales per day with final Total sum for the site total.
Other report column headers and the Report legend has been updated to reflect these changes.
NXT-71622.desc:
Currently, As a left over from adopting PrimeroEdge ways, the report is including credit card sums with cash.
This causes issues for our customers when trying to make clear and accurate reporting of cash amounts.

Thus we will need to modify the Cash Collection Report to remove card transactions entirely, so only relevant payment types are displayed.

{color:#008da6}Post Note after call discussion:{color}
The goal of this report is to show Cash amounts, unfortunately checks will be tied to this as breaking checks out further complicates things out of scope.
Thus this report will be changed to remove Credit cards for sums and calculations involving cash and we will include a new report option to simply display credit card amounts like we do with refunds.
We will also update the report legend to accomodate the changes.

Users will come to expect that this report appropriately deals with their cash and checks as it always has while avoiding mix-ups with Credit amounts. Breaking things out further will bloat and complicate this report unnecessarily.

NXT-67032.rn:
*Attendance SchoolCafe*



New System Setting: Calculate Attendance Factor from Daily Attendance Records

A new system setting has been added to allow user to determine if daily attendance counts will be utilized in Edit Checks, reporting and more.

-Default off

When off, the system will utilize entered Attendance factors and ADA (Average Daily Attendance) by month.
When on, the system will utilize entered Daily Attendance counts and calculate Attedance Factor per day and Average by month based on entered counts. This option will also make the checkbox available to utilize Attendance counts in various reporting as well as add additional Edit Check functionality and an end of day task. 


!Pasted Graphic 5-20260105-193315.png|width=448,alt="Pasted Graphic 5-20260105-193315.png"!

Accountability > Attendance

Monthly and Daily



You can now track attendance in School Cafe To be used in both Edit Checks and Reporting.

There are two modes of attendance tracking available. Monthly Allows tracking a count across a whole month. This is useful for those who really only need to track things at the end of the month. Using the Daily option will allow for the most accuracy in Edit Checks and ADA/ADP reporting.

Bulk Update:
The bulk update box will allow users to quickly apply a ADA (Average Daily Attendance) and/or an AF (Attendance Factor) across all months at one time. This will allow a quick way to get data in to compare against for the ADA report.

Other Tools:


!Pasted Graphic 1-20260105-163119.png|width=448,alt="Pasted Graphic 1-20260105-163119.png"!

Unsaved Change Indicator - a red dot within the grid to indicate a unsaved change is present



Highlight Missing Counts - For users who commonly want to work in a bigger picture mode we have added this function to bring blank values to attention.

Export - Export Grid Data with applied filters
Include Weekends - shows/hides weekend fields. (Daily only)



Edit Check Reports:


!Pasted Graphic-20260105-155122.png|width=448,alt="Pasted Graphic-20260105-155122.png"!

For both reports when the attendance setting is selecting the “Use Attendance Counts”



Edit Check Report
When the check box to Use Attendance Counts is checked the Edit Check report will Calculate the Attendance Factor by the average of selected days for the report.





Edit Check Worksheet


!Edit Check Work Sheet-20260105-194233.png|width=448,alt="Edit Check Work Sheet-20260105-194233.png"!

When the check box to Use Attendance Counts is checked the Editcheck Worksheet will display 3 new columns (Attendance, Eligible Count, and Calculated Attendance Factor) as well as 2 new rows per mealtype for Average and Subtotals. The Report will also calculate Attendance Factor utilizing entered data on the Attendance page for the Daily entry method.

When run for multiple days the overall attendance factor will be the average of calculated Attendance Factors per day across serving days.


Edit Checks:


!Pasted Graphic 3-20260105-171954.png|width=448,alt="Pasted Graphic 3-20260105-171954.png"!

When the daily entry method is active for the Attendance entry the Edit Checks feature will add a new check for when the entered attendance exceeds the enrolled student count per site. The Edit check will also modify when the meal counts exceeds the attendance factor using the actual attendance count for attendance factor (Attendance Count/Approved Count = AF converted to percentage). Users can also resolve the new edit check from the Edit Checks screen if needed.

ADA/ADP Reports:


!ADA ADP Report-20260105-192712.png|width=448,alt="ADA ADP Report-20260105-192712.png"!

The ADA ADP (Average Daily Attendance and Average Daily Participation) Report has been added to the reports page.

Use this report to view Meal Participation in relation to Attendance, and important metric to track for operation effeciency.


End of Day:
When the setting is enabled a new section has been added for Attendance Count on the End of Day section where a user can see if an attendance count has been entered for the day as well as enter it there without having to navigate to the Attendance page.


!Pasted Graphic 2-20260105-171319.png|width=448,alt="Pasted Graphic 2-20260105-171319.png"!

Specific Page and Special Configuration Permissions have also been added for this new functionality.

Page Permissions

Special Permissions
